hypothesis: Enhancing steric complementarity with SSTR2-unique ECL2 (res 192/193/195/197) and TM5 (205/208/209/212) by introducing bulky aromatic residues at position 5 and hydrophobic residues at position 11 will improve SSTR2 selectivity (Δmargin > 0) by avoiding off-target SSTR3/5 interactions.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current designs are strong SSTR2 binders but still exhibit off-target interactions, particularly at conserved pocket residues in SSTR3 and SSTR5. The next iteration should focus on disrupting these interactions at non-pharmacophore positions to enhance selectivity.

proposed_changes.rationale: These positions are non-pharmacophore and are likely involved in off-target interactions (especially with SSTR3 and SSTR5). Focusing mutations here can disrupt off-target binding while preserving key interactions at positions 7-10 (FWKT) and Cys3/Cys14.
These mutations are designed to reduce hydrophobic or hydrogen-bonding interactions at off-target interfaces while maintaining flexibility and compatibility with SSTR2 binding.
